description_long: This saxophone features a classic, curved brass body with a prominent, flared bell and a series of rounded keys and pads distributed along its length. The instrument is finished in a metallic gold color, giving it a polished and professional appearance. The neck is smoothly curved, leading to a black mouthpiece at the top, which is securely attached. The keywork is detailed, with several keys and rods visible, including side keys and palm keys, all designed for ergonomic playability. The bell opening is wide and slightly angled, with a smooth rim and no visible decorative engravings. Overall, the saxophone exudes a sense of craftsmanship and is visually striking, suitable for use in urban musical performances or street scenes.
description: A gold, polished saxophone with a curved neck, black mouthpiece, and detailed keywork along its body. The wide, flared bell and smooth finish make it visually striking.
description_view_0: The saxophone is seen from a three-quarter angle, highlighting the curved neck, black mouthpiece, and the main body with its keywork and flared bell.
description_view_1: A direct front view shows the circular, flared bell opening and the alignment of the main tube, with the mouthpiece visible at the top.
description_view_2: The opposite three-quarter view reveals the other side of the keywork and the full curvature of the neck, with the bell angled outward.
description_view_3: A side profile emphasizes the length of the saxophone, the arrangement of keys along the body, and the smooth, continuous curve from neck to bell.
attribute_bell_opening: The bell opening is circular, flared, and oriented slightly outward from the main body. The diameter is approximately 12 centimeters, with a smooth rim and no unique features.
attribute_key_layout: The saxophone has a series of rounded keys and pads distributed along the main tube, including side keys and palm keys. The keywork appears complete and follows a standard saxophone layout.
attribute_neck_curve: The neck is smoothly curved with a gentle, continuous bend leading to the mouthpiece. It appears detachable, as is typical for saxophones, and has no unusual features.
